articles.title: Gold rallies to over two-week high, eyes $4,150 as traders track US-Iran diplomacy efforts
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) rallies to an over two-week high, around the $4,140-$4,141 area, during the Asian session on Wednesday amid hopes that US-Iran diplomacy could ease energy prices and temper hawkish US Federal Reserve (Fed) expectations.

articles.title: EUR/JPY Price Forecast: Tests ascending triangle top above 186.00
articles.expand: EUR/JPY extends its gains for the second successive day, trading around 186.20 during the Asian hours on Wednesday. The currency cross is retaining a bullish near-term bias as it holds above both the nine-period and 50-period Exponential Moving Averages (EMAs).

articles.title: FX Watch: AUD/NZD and AUD/CAD Setups for an Australian Jobs Miss
articles.expand: Another miss in Australia’s employment data could erase RBA tightening expectations for the remainder of the year. Here’s what I’m watching on AUD/NZD and AUD/CAD if this happens.

articles.title: FX Watch: AUD/USD and AUD/NZD Eye Breakouts on Potential Australian Jobs Beat
articles.expand: Australia is dropping its June employment numbers! If the report hits or exceeds estimates, AUD/USD and AUD/NZD may be worth a second look.

articles.title: Japan trade data shows exports in June higher than expected
articles.expand: Just the data in this post. I'll have more to come on this separately. ADDED, more here: Japan's oil-driven import surge widens trade gap, complicates BOJ hold ﻿ Earlier: USD/JPY hits fresh 40-year high above 163 as equities, yields and oil all gain Exports to China +17.6% year/year exports to Asia +22.7% year/year exports to U.S. +13% year/year exports to EU +20.3% year/year This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.

articles.title: Japan's oil-driven import surge widens trade gap, complicates BOJ hold
articles.expand: The scale of the import beat, more than 4 percentage points above forecast, reinforces the case that the BOJ will need to maintain its tightening bias even while holding rates next week, keeping USD/JPY intervention risk and rate differential trades in focus. The divergence between falling crude volumes and a 59.3% jump in yen value terms underscores how much of this inflation pressure is currency-driven rather than demand-driven, meaning a stronger yen would do more to ease the import bill than any plausible near-term shift in oil demand. On the export side, resilience tied to AI-linked data ...

articles.title: Dollar Edges Higher as Crude Oil Prices and Bond Yields Climb
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) rose by +0.23% on Tuesday. The dollar moved higher on Tuesday as the escalation of hostilities between the US and Iran is boosting crude oil prices, which raises inflation expectations that could prompt the Fed to tighten monetary policy, a supportive factor for the dollar....

articles.title: UK CPI set to show receding inflation in June as GBP/USD fails at May highs
articles.expand: The UK Office for National Statistics (ONS) will release the June Consumer Price Index (CPI) figures on Wednesday at 06:00 GMT, a print that will matter for markets. Consensus expectations point to inflation pressures still above the Bank of England’s (BoE) target, although losing further momentum.

articles.title: Canadian Dollar edges higher amid bullish oil prices; upside potential seems limited
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair struggles to capitalize on its strong weekly gains registered over the past two days and edges lower during the Asian session on Wednesday. Spot prices currently trade around the 1.4100 round figure, though the fundamental backdrop warrants caution for aggressive bearish traders.

articles.title: Australian Dollar strengthens above 0.7000 on RBA hawkish stance
articles.expand: The AUD/USD pair gathers strength to near 0.7010 during the early Asian trading hours on Wednesday. The Australian Dollar (AUD) strengthens against the US Dollar (USD) as the likelihood of the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) interest rate hike rises.

articles.title: Euro holds gains above 1.1400 on hawkish ECB expectations despite US-Iran tensions
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair trades with mild gains around 1.1405 during the early Asian session on Wednesday. A hawkish tone from the European Central Bank (ECB) provides some support to the Euro (EUR) against the US Dollar (USD). Traders await the upcoming ECB interest rate decision on Thursday.

articles.title: GBP/JPY Price Forecast: Holds above 218.00 bulls eye YTD peak
articles.expand: The GBP/JPY consolidates around 218.20 as the Pound Sterling loses momentum amid the new PM, Andy Burnham, taking office. Fears of a possible intervention by Japanese authorities capped the cross-pair advance, which remains trading near year-to-date (YTD) highs seen on July 15.

articles.title: Preview: ECB set to hold rates in July, hints of September hike expected: ING
articles.expand: ING's scenario analysis frames a hawkish-leaning hold as the base case, with EUR/USD seen holding around 1.140 and 10-year Bund yields near 3.15% under that outcome. A more dovish tilt would likely pull EUR/USD back toward 1.130 and Bund yields toward 3.05%, while a surprise 25bp hike could push EUR/USD to 1.150 and Bund yields to 3.20%. ING said rates continue to take their cue from oil, with a September hike already close to fully priced in barring an easing in crude prices. On FX, the bank sees a hawkish ECB as necessary but not sufficient to keep EUR/USD above 1.140, with a retest of the J...

articles.expand: Secretary of War Hegseth needs more money for war Crude oil futures settle at $84.91 Tension in the middle east intensifies Apple Reinvents the upgrade cycle. articles.title: Federal Reserve seen holding rates next week amid Oil shock ‒ Reuters
articles.expand: A Reuters poll revealed that the Federal Reserve is most likely to keep interest rates unchanged for the rest of the year as it battles stubbornly high inflation that has remained above the Fed’s 2% goal for at least 5 years.

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Holding on to 0.7000
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar's recovery appears to have met some resistance just above the 0.7000 threshold against the US Dollar so far. Indeed, AUD/USD still struggles to surpass that mark in a convincing fashion, while the constructive outlook appears unchanged above its critical 200-day SMA near 0.6890.

articles.title: AUDUSD reached key technical target and backed off. MA target approached on the downside
articles.expand: The AUDUSD moved lower into the start of trading today, but once again found willing buyers against its rising 100-hour moving average. That is an important development because the pair also attracted buyers at that same technical level during yesterday's Asia-Pacific session. In other words, the last two tests of the 100-hour moving average have been defended by buyers, reinforcing its importance as a key short-term support level. The rebound from today's low extended from 0.6991 to 0.7026, carrying the pair above several significant technical hurdles. The rally pushed through last week's hig...

articles.title: GBP/USD Falls After Cabinet Changes
articles.expand: By Analytical Department RoboForex GBP/USD fell to 1.3437 on Tuesday as investors assessed the appointment of Andy Burnham as the new Prime Minister of the UK and the outlook for monetary policy. Burnham succeeded Keir Starmer without a contest, becoming the country’s seventh prime minister in the past decade and the second since the Labour […]

articles.title: XAU/USD Price forecast: Middle East crisis intensifies, Gold up
articles.expand: The US Dollar (USD) trades with a firmer tone on Tuesday, but so does Gold. The bright metal is moving away from the $4,000 mark and trades closer to an intraday high of $4,084 than to the psychological threshold.

articles.title: NZ Inflation Tops RBNZ’s Own Forecast
articles.expand: New Zealand’s inflation hit 4.1% in the June 2026 quarter, beating forecasts and the RBNZ’s own estimate, as petrol and diesel prices drove most of the increase.

articles.title: Dollar Supported by Higher Crude Prices
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) is up by +0.06% today. The dollar is slightly higher today as the escalation of hostilities between the US and Iran is boosting crude oil prices, which raises inflation expectations that could prompt the Fed to tighten monetary policy, a supportive factor for the dollar....

articles.title: USDJPY trades to a new 40 year high
articles.expand: The USDJPY is extending to the upside once again, supported by rising U.S. Treasury yields and persistent demand for the U.S. dollar. The benchmark 10-year Treasury yield is up 3.4 basis points to 4.632%, while the 2-year yield has climbed 3.2 basis points to 4.246%, helping to widen the interest rate advantage in favor of the dollar. Technically, the move has carried the pair above the July 1 high at 162.833, pushing USDJPY to its highest level in nearly 40 years. The session high has reached 162.89, keeping buyers firmly in control of the near-term trend. As highlighted in today's Kickstart ...

articles.title: UK Unemployment Holds Steady at 4.9%
articles.expand: UK unemployment held at 4.9% and wage growth cooled to a four-year low, landing after a new PM took office and days before the BoE’s rate call.

articles.title: USDCAD moves to new highs and tests MA target
articles.expand: The USDCAD is pushing to a new session high, bringing the pair to a key technical inflection point as buyers test the falling 200-hour moving average, currently at 1.40858. That level has taken on added importance after serving as the catalyst for the latest downside correction. Recall that the pair broke below the 200-hour moving average on July 8, shifting the short-term bias in favor of sellers and triggering a steady decline that ultimately carried the price to a low just above the 1.4000 level yesterday. ﻿﻿﻿ The tone began to change during the North American session yesterday when buyers ...

articles.title: Brent Above $91 Brings USD/JPY Back Within Sight of 40-Year High
articles.expand: Markets struggled to settle on a single narrative today as investors weighed conflicting geopolitical headlines alongside a range of regional developments. Reports that mediators had proposed a 10-day ceasefire between Washington and Tehran initially offered hope that last month’s Memorandum of Understanding could be revived. Those hopes were tempered, however, by market chatter that US […] The post Brent Above $91 Brings USD/JPY Back Within Sight of 40-Year High appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: The USD is mixed vs the major currencies. In the kickstart video, I outline the key technical levels in play
articles.expand: The USD is mixed to little changed vs the three major currency pairs - the EURUSD, USDJPY and GBPUSD. In the video above, I speak to the technicals that are helping to drive the price action for each. All traders should understand the bias, the risk and the targets for trades. I define those levels for each and explain and show why from a technical perspective in the video. Kickstart the North American session by understanding the roadmap. Meanwhile, UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham's proposal to cut VAT on household energy bills is already facing scrutiny over how it will be financed. The gover...

articles.title: Aided by China, Equities Bounce Back, and the Canadian Dollar Recovers from the Drop on US Threat of 50% Tariffs
articles.expand: The US dollar is mostly softer against the major and emerging market currencies. The yen is an exception, and the market is pushing the dollar closer to the 40-year high recorded at the start of the month near JPY162.85. Oil prices are firm as the de-escalation efforts are proving tricky. Meanwhile, reports that Beijing is supporting the equity market by mobilizing state-backed investors may have encouraged bargain hunting after the tech and chip pullback. Late yesterday, the US threatened 50% tariffs on some Canadian goods for actions it claims discriminate against US companies. At first the ...

articles.title: Eurozone banks tighten lending standards further as geopolitical risks weigh on credit conditions
articles.expand: Full report here The ECB's Bank Lending Survey showed that euro area banks continued to tighten lending standards in the second quarter, reflecting heightened concerns over the economic outlook, geopolitical tensions and energy-related risks. Banks reported a moderate tightening of credit standards for corporate loans (net 7%), as well as for housing loans (9%) and consumer credit (12%). The main drivers were weaker risk tolerance and higher perceived credit risks. Banks expect lending standards to tighten further across all loan categories in the third quarter. Banks also tightened overall le...

articles.title: Trouble already for Burnham in his first policy announcement?
articles.expand: I guess that is how quickly things can unravel in Westminster. From earlier: UK prime minister Burnham announces VAT cut from energy bills to bring down cost of living As mentioned in the announcement earlier, the policy cost of cutting VAT from energy bills would be around £850 million. And it was reported that it will be funded through the now cancelled digital ID scheme, which was estimated to cost approximately £1.8 billion over the coming years. However, UK lawmaker Darren Jones is now out to dispute that notion in saying that the digital ID scheme was in fact unfunded in the first place....

articles.title: Iran says any decision to reopen the Strait of Hormuz depends on security considerations
articles.expand: As a reminder, the Strait of Hormuz has reverted back to a de facto closure as the US and Iran are engaging in military strikes once again. And with that, the US has also reimposed their naval blockade at the other end of the strait. As such, both sides have pretty much hit the reset button to the ceasefire deal/memorandum of understanding. For the time being, the latest shiptracking data shows that traffic along the strait has gone back all the way down. And by that, I mean to the lowest point during the peak of the conflict. In terms of verified crossings, there are less than 10 ships transi...

articles.title: UK unemployment rate holds steady in May, payrolls decline again in June
articles.expand: May ILO unemployment rate 4.9% vs 5.0% expected Prior 4.9% May employment change 147k vs 80k expected Prior 100k May average weekly earnings +4.3% vs +4.5% 3m/y expected Prior +4.4% May average weekly earnings (ex bonus) +3.4% vs +3.4% 3m/y expected Prior +3.4% June payrolls change -4k Prior 2k; revised to 3k As a reminder, the numbers here are still based on the Labour Force Survey (LFS) with that having data quality issues as the survey transition is delayed . And that remains the number one caveat that ONS is pinning on their labour market report this month. The headline estimate points to ...

articles.title: UK prime minister Burnham announces VAT cut from energy bills to bring down cost of living
articles.expand: Burnham a man with a plan? Or is this just a move to try and bolster popularity early on in his tenure? In his first notable policy action in charge, he has scrapped VAT on energy bills starting from 1 October. The move will cost roughly £850 million and be funded through the now cancelled digital ID scheme. For some context, the previously proposed digital ID scheme was estimated to cost approximately £1.8 billion over the coming years. "I said I wanted to give people breathing space, and that's what I'm announcing on my second day as prime minister. We're taking immediate action to cut taxes...

articles.title: RBNZ's own inflation model, Q2 2026: 2.7% y/y, unchanged from Q1
articles.expand: Unchanged from Q1 takes some pressure out of the inflaiton readings today. More detail: RBNZ's sectoral factor model, its preferred underlying inflation measure. Earlier: New Zealand's Q2 CPI rose 4.1% y/y and 1.5% q/q, both abo﻿﻿ve forecasts and the RBNZ's own 3.9% estimate.﻿﻿ This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.

articles.title: Event Guide: U.K. CPI Report (June 2026)
articles.expand: The U.K.’s June inflation could reset BOE expectations, but Sterling trades may still depend on the broader risk mood.

articles.title: NZD is solidly higher after higher than expected CPI. Be aware there is RBNZ inflation data due soon.
articles.expand: New Zealand's Q2 CPI rose 4.1% y/y and 1.5% q/q, both above forecasts and the RBNZ's own 3.9% estimate. Its sent the kiwi $ higher on the session. Still to come is the RBNZ's sectoral factor model, its preferred underlying inflation measure. Due at 3pm New Zealand time today, which is 3am GMT and 11pm US Eastern time on Monday. This will markets a further read on the extent of underlying price pressure. The model is the RBNZ's own preferred measure of core inflation, built by extracting a common inflation signal from a large number of individual CPI components rather than relying on the headli...

articles.title: New Zealand CPI Hits 4.1% as Fuel Costs Reinforce RBNZ Challenge
articles.expand: New Zealand’s consumer prices rose 1.5% qoq in the second quarter, lifting annual inflation to 4.1% from 3.1%, in line with market expectations. The acceleration was driven overwhelmingly by energy-related costs, with petrol prices surging 20.1% over the quarter and contributing nearly half of the quarterly increase in the Consumer Price Index. Prices for other […] The post New Zealand CPI Hits 4.1% as Fuel Costs Reinforce RBNZ Challenge appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: FX strategists eye ECB hawkishness as Middle East risk lifts euro case
articles.expand: Currency strategists broadly agree the ECB's tone this week matters more for the euro than the widely expected hold itself, with the degree of hawkishness on further hikes seen as the key swing factor against the dollar. A clear signal that the Bank remains willing to act beyond September is viewed as the main defence against renewed euro weakness if the Middle East conflict escalates further and pushes energy prices higher. Rate markets have already moved to price in close to two additional hikes by year end, lifting short term euro area yields back near their highs for the year. Softer US in...

articles.title: Preview - ECB seen holding at 2.25% as Middle East conflict clouds outlook, September hike still in play
articles.expand: A hold this week would leave the door open for one more move in September, keeping the deposit rate on a gradual path toward the upper end of what TD views as neutral. Markets are likely to parse Lagarde's press conference closely for any shift in how policymakers characterise the economic scenario, given the explicit link the bank draws between forward guidance and how the Middle East conflict evolves. A prolonged or intensifying conflict would likely be read as skewing risks toward the more hawkish scenario TD outlines, supporting the case for September action, while a de-escalation could re...

articles.title: NZ inflation jumps to 4.1% in Q2, beating forecasts and RBNZ's own estimate, with more to come!
articles.expand: A hotter than expected print raises the odds the RBNZ needs to extend its recently restarted tightening cycle beyond what markets currently price. With the cash rate only just lifted to 2.50% after three years on hold, today's data strengthens the case for follow through hikes and is likely to support the New Zealand dollar and push short end rates higher. Attention will now turn quickly to the RBNZ's sectoral factor model due later today, given headline CPI was so heavily skewed by fuel. A hot core reading would reinforce the hawkish case; a soft one could temper it. --- New Zealand inflation...

articles.title: Preview - Deutsche Bank sees ECB pausing this week before a September rate rise
articles.expand: A widely expected pause this week would leave the deposit rate at 2.25%, with attention shifting quickly to how the Governing Council frames the balance of risks around energy and inflation. Market pricing for the endpoint of this tightening cycle has swung meaningfully in recent weeks, dropping to 2.50% when oil prices fell sharply in June before rebounding to 2.75% as Middle East tensions escalated again. That volatility leaves scope for a hawkish surprise if incoming data, including today's flash PMIs, point to persistent price pressure building beneath the headline inflation numbers. A dov...

articles.title: BNZ tips New Zealand inflation to blow past RBNZ's own forecast
articles.expand: An inflation print above the RBNZ's 3.9% projection would strengthen the case for further tightening, reinforcing the higher-for-longer rates narrative already building in NZ swap and bond markets. Traders will watch the core measures as closely as the headline figure, given their weight in the Bank's policy calculus. A hot core reading would likely extend the recent widening in NZ-global rate spreads and could add support to the New Zealand dollar. Conversely, any downside surprise in the core gauges could temper hawkish repricing even if the headline number beats expectations. --- BNZ believ...

articles.title: Economic and event calendar in Asia Tuesday, July 21, 2026 - New Zealand inflation data
articles.expand: Q2 held the peak of the global oil price shock and as such the NZ data is expected to sh﻿ow elevated CPI. Given the renewed conflict its not going to be possible to shrug it off and look though it. I'll get a preview of this data posted separately. In brief, any further upside to these numbers beyond expectations should provide a bid for the kiwi $. Note, there is further inflation data due from New Zealand, no shown in the screenshot beloe. This is the Reserve Bank of New Zealand's own sectoral factor model (core inflation) at 3pm NZ time (0300 GMT/ 2300 US Eastern time). This article was wri...

articles.title: GBPUSD break the 200 hour moving average and moves toward a cluster of technical targets
articles.expand: The GBPUSD is extending to the downside, and in the process the technical picture has shifted more decisively in favor of the sellers. What had been a relatively balanced market earlier in the session has evolved into one where key support levels have been broken, giving sellers greater confidence and putting buyers on the defensive. The first important technical victory came when the pair moved below its 100-hour moving average, currently at 1.34585. That moving average had been acting as a near-term barometer for the short-term trend in the Asian early European market, and once the price sli...

articles.title: The AUDUSD and NZDUSD are moving higher today helped by risk-on flow and technicals
articles.expand: The AUDUSD and NZDUSD are both moving higher to start the North American session, supported by an improving risk-on backdrop and constructive technical developments. U.S. equity futures are pointing to a stronger open, with S P 500 futures up roughly 21 points and Nasdaq futures ahead by about 252 points. Helping sentiment are reports that discussions are continuing around a possible 10-day cease-fire in the Middle East, along with renewed optimism that the Strait of Hormuz could reopen to normal shipping traffic. Those headlines have encouraged investors to move back toward risk-sensitive ass...

articles.title: Canada Inflation Cools Sharply as Lower Gasoline Prices Pull Headline CPI Below Expectations
articles.expand: Canada’s annual inflation rate slowed more than expected to 2.8% in June from 3.2% in May, while headline CPI fell -0.4% on the month, marking the largest monthly decline since December 2024. The moderation was driven primarily by gasoline prices, which remained elevated from a year earlier but rose at a much slower annual pace […] The post Canada Inflation Cools Sharply as Lower Gasoline Prices Pull Headline CPI Below Expectations appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: Canadian Dollar Futures: The Loonie Fights to Hold a Critical Support Level Near 0.71
articles.expand: The Canadian dollar futures contract, widely known as the loonie, remains locked in a broader consolidation range between roughly 0.71 and 0.74 as traders weigh a fresh round of North American trade uncertainty against a firmer commodity backdrop. The Bank of Canada held its policy rate at 2.25% on July 15, its sixth consecutive hold, while trimming its 2026 growth forecast even as it pointed to a recovery later in the year. Meanwhile, the July 1 CUSMA review deadline passed without the United States agreeing to a full extension, leaving existing tariffs in place and shifting the pact toward a...

articles.title: EURGBP Extends Lower Within Final Wave E as Bearish Momentum Builds
articles.expand: EURGBP is extending lower as anticipated after completing a larger corrective structure, with the pair now trading within the final wave E of a long-term triangle pattern. The current decline could continue toward the 0.8400–0.8300 support zone, while any short-term recovery should remain corrective.

articles.title: PRA fines HDI Global SE £4,165,000 for inaccurate reporting of FSCS Liabilities and FSCS Fee Tariff data
articles.expand: The Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA) has imposed a financial penalty of £4,165,000 on HDI Global SE in connection with the submission of incorrect data to the PRA.

articles.title: Commodity Currencies Rise As Crude Oil Prices Climb
articles.expand: The commodity currencies such as Australia, the New Zealand and the Canadian dollars strengthened against their major currencies in the European session on Monday, as the crude oil prices surged after the hostilities between the U.S. and Iran intensified raising the possibility of a full-scale conflict and closing the Strait of Hormuz to maritime traffic.

articles.title: ECB SAFE Survey: Loan Conditions Tighten While Inflation Expectations Hold Steady
articles.expand: Euro area firms reported tighter borrowing conditions in the second quarter, even as inflation expectations remained broadly stable and cost pressures continued to ease, according to the ECB’s latest Survey on the Access to Finance of Enterprises (SAFE). A net 42% of firms reported higher bank loan interest rates, up sharply from 26% in the […] The post ECB SAFE Survey: Loan Conditions Tighten While Inflation Expectations Hold Steady appeared first on ActionForex .
